questions.question: What is Node.js?
options: B) JavaScript runtime built on V8 engine enabling server-side JS execution outside browser
questions.correctAnswer: B) JavaScript runtime built on V8 engine enabling server-side JS execution outside browser

questions.question: What is the Node.js event loop?
options: B) Single-threaded loop processing I/O callbacks from libuv thread pool — enables non-blocking I/O
questions.correctAnswer: B) Single-threaded loop processing I/O callbacks from libuv thread pool — enables non-blocking I/O

questions.question: What is the difference between require() and import?
options: B) require() is CommonJS (synchronous, dynamic); import is ES modules (static, async, tree-shakeable)
D) require() is for JSON only
questions.correctAnswer: B) require() is CommonJS (synchronous, dynamic); import is ES modules (static, async, tree-shakeable)

questions.question: What is process.env used for?
options: B) Accessing environment variables — used for secrets, config, API keys without hardcoding
questions.correctAnswer: B) Accessing environment variables — used for secrets, config, API keys without hardcoding

questions.question: What is the purpose of package.json?
options: B) Project manifest defining name, version, dependencies, scripts, and metadata
questions.correctAnswer: B) Project manifest defining name, version, dependencies, scripts, and metadata

questions.question: What is the difference between dependencies and devDependencies?
options: B) dependencies are needed in production; devDependencies only needed during development/testing
questions.correctAnswer: B) dependencies are needed in production; devDependencies only needed during development/testing

questions.question: What is middleware in Express?
options: B) Function(req, res, next) that runs between request and response — for auth, logging, parsing
questions.correctAnswer: B) Function(req, res, next) that runs between request and response — for auth, logging, parsing

questions.question: What does next() do in Express middleware?
options: A) Goes to next route file
B) Passes control to next middleware in stack — must call it to continue chain
questions.correctAnswer: B) Passes control to next middleware in stack — must call it to continue chain

questions.question: What is a Node.js stream?
options: B) Abstract interface for reading/writing data in chunks — memory efficient for large files
questions.correctAnswer: B) Abstract interface for reading/writing data in chunks — memory efficient for large files

questions.question: What is the EventEmitter class?
options: B) Core class enabling event-driven architecture — on() to listen, emit() to trigger events
questions.correctAnswer: B) Core class enabling event-driven architecture — on() to listen, emit() to trigger events

questions.question: What is cluster module in Node.js?
options: B) Creates child processes sharing server port — utilizes multiple CPU cores since Node is single-threaded
questions.correctAnswer: B) Creates child processes sharing server port — utilizes multiple CPU cores since Node is single-threaded

questions.question: What is the purpose of __dirname?
options: B) Absolute path of directory containing current module file
questions.correctAnswer: B) Absolute path of directory containing current module file

questions.question: What is CORS and how is it handled in Express?
options: B) Cross-Origin Resource Sharing — browser security policy; handled with cors middleware or manual headers
questions.correctAnswer: B) Cross-Origin Resource Sharing — browser security policy; handled with cors middleware or manual headers

questions.question: What is JWT (JSON Web Token)?
options: B) Compact self-contained token for auth — header.payload.signature, stateless, verified with secret
questions.correctAnswer: B) Compact self-contained token for auth — header.payload.signature, stateless, verified with secret

questions.question: What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous file reading in Node?
options: B) Sync (readFileSync) blocks event loop; async (readFile) uses callback and doesn't block
questions.correctAnswer: B) Sync (readFileSync) blocks event loop; async (readFile) uses callback and doesn't block

questions.question: What is rate limiting in APIs?
options: B) Controlling number of requests per user/IP in time window — prevents abuse and DDoS
questions.correctAnswer: B) Controlling number of requests per user/IP in time window — prevents abuse and DDoS

questions.question: What is the purpose of helmet.js?
options: B) Express middleware setting various HTTP security headers to protect against common vulnerabilities
questions.correctAnswer: B) Express middleware setting various HTTP security headers to protect against common vulnerabilities

questions.question: What is bcrypt used for?
options: B) Password hashing with salt — slow by design to prevent brute force attacks
questions.correctAnswer: B) Password hashing with salt — slow by design to prevent brute force attacks

questions.question: What is the purpose of .env files?
options: B) Store environment variables locally — loaded with dotenv, never committed to version control
questions.correctAnswer: B) Store environment variables locally — loaded with dotenv, never committed to version control

questions.question: What is graceful shutdown in Node.js?
options: B) Listening for SIGTERM signal to finish existing requests before stopping — prevents data loss
questions.correctAnswer: B) Listening for SIGTERM signal to finish existing requests before stopping — prevents data loss

questions.question: Which runtime is used for Node.js?
questions.correctAnswer: A) V8 Engine

questions.question: Which Node.js module handles file system?
questions.correctAnswer: B) fs
